task_completion.description: Create a local generic repository named 'tradex-artifacts'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Artifactory Repositories list or configuration page shows a local generic repository named 'tradex-artifacts' with the description 'TradeX Platform build artifacts'.

task_completion.description: Create a local Maven repository named 'tradex-maven-releases'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Artifactory Repositories list or configuration page shows a local Maven repository named 'tradex-maven-releases' with the description 'TradeX Java release artifacts'.
The Artifactory Identity and Access (or Groups) page shows a group named 'tradex-developers' has been created.

task_completion.description: Create a permission target granting specific privileges to the group on both repositories
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Permissions page shows a target named 'tradex-dev-perms' that includes both 'tradex-artifacts' and 'tradex-maven-releases' repositories, and grants Admin, Deploy, Annotate, and Read privileges to the 'tradex-developers' group.

task_completion.description: Generate an access token for the admin user
task_completion.visual_evidence: The User Profile or Access Tokens page shows a generated token with the description 'TradeX CI/CD production token'.

task_completion.description: Upload the commons-io jar file to the 'tradex-artifacts' repository
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Artifacts Tree or Browser shows 'commons-io-2.15.1.jar' successfully uploaded and residing inside the 'tradex-artifacts' repository.

integrity.description: The agent used the Artifactory web UI to complete the setup rather than using terminal API/curl commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show navigation and form filling within the JFrog Artifactory web interface in a browser, rather than terminal windows executing curl commands.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Artifactory application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar shows 'http://localhost:8082' and the interface matches JFrog Artifactory.

integrity.description: The agent uploaded the actual file from the specified directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file upload dialog or browser interaction shows the agent navigating to '/home/ga/Desktop/' to select 'commons-io-2.15.1.jar'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: The Artifactory URL is http://localhost:8082. The target repositories to be created are 'tradex-artifacts' (Generic) and 'tradex-maven-releases' (Maven). The target group is 'tradex-developers'. The permission target is 'tradex-dev-perms'. The token description must be exactly 'TradeX CI/CD production token'. The artifact to upload is 'commons-io-2.15.1.jar' located at '/home/ga/Desktop/commons-io-2.15.1.jar', and it must be uploaded specifically to the 'tradex-artifacts' repository.
